# Questions in Livestream 48

Live Stream #48: Good Friday Orthodox Style.

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=683" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:23</a> | In Psalm 22:16, should the text read "pierced," "like a lion," or something combining both ideas? | He treats the textual issue as complex and works through the Hebrew evidence rather than giving a simplistic one-word answer. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=936" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">15:36</a> | What is the most likely year and season for Jesus's birth? | He says the year can be narrowed more confidently than the month, while some seasonal proposals remain tentative. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=1095" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">18:15</a> | What do you think of the Shroud of Turin and the newer scattering-data arguments for an older date? | He remains unconvinced that it is Christ's shroud. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=1297" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">21:37</a> | What does Genesis 2:5 mean, and does it support young-earth creationist claims? | He says the passage needs to be read in context and does not think the common YEC use of it is compelling. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=1544" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">25:44</a> | Did Revelation 19:11-14 happen around 66-70 CE, as some preterists argue from Josephus and Tacitus? | He says the proposal does not work for him. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=1831" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:31</a> | Is there evidence that Jews lived in Egypt in the sixth century BCE, as reflected in Jeremiah 44? | He points to evidence for Israelite/Judahite communities in Egypt and treats Jeremiah's setting as historically plausible. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=2365" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:25</a> | Do Egyptian daybooks provide a precedent for Jacob's family keeping written records in Avaris or Goshen? | He says Egyptian record-keeping does provide useful background, and he discusses how such documents functioned. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=2846" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">47:26</a> | How is the temple motif woven into Genesis 1-2, and what should I read on it? | He endorses the temple-reading approach and recommends John Walton as a helpful starting point. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=3226" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">53:46</a> | Did the magicians in Exodus 7 use magic, sleight of hand, or is it myth? | He is open to magic or sleight of hand, rejects myth, and says deriving sleight of hand from "hidden" is an etymological fallacy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=3465" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">57:45</a> | Did ancient Egypt have an ablution ritual like baptism? | Yes; Egyptian priestly purification included ritual bathing, clean linens, sandals, and natron mouth washing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=3670" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:01:10</a> | Was Noah polygamous, and how would that fit with the eight souls on the ark? | He treats Noah's polygamy as a long-shot possibility, not his preferred view, and says "eight" may not strictly exclude more people. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=3964" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:06:04</a> | Did Egypt have a strong naval presence in the Mediterranean and shipbuilding beyond Nile transport? | Egypt had a strong Nile navy and Mediterranean shipping and trade, but not a large Mediterranean military navy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=4328" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:12:08</a> | Why do Orthodox Bibles have more Old Testament chapters or books, and who removed them? | It depends on which Orthodox canon; differences come from Septuagint and apocryphal traditions and expanded book forms. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=4524" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:15:24</a> | Why was Moses worried about being stoned for sacrificing what was an abomination to Egyptians in Exodus 8:26? | Sheep or goat sacrifice would offend Egyptian purity expectations, so Moses gives that as the reason for going away from Egypt. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=4755" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:19:15</a> | What are your favorite Old and New Testament books? | Exodus for the Old Testament; John for the New Testament. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=5402" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:30:02</a> | What are your thoughts on Torah observance for Messianic believers? | Keep the weightier matters and spirit of Torah; Christ fulfilled the ceremonial and sacrificial burden, so kosher and similar observance is not required. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=5984" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:39:44</a> | What are your thoughts on the exclusivity of priesthood in Genesis 47:22? | He connects it to Egyptian priestly land endowments that kings generally did not reclaim. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=6411" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:46:51</a> | Was the covenant made with Israel an ancient suzerain-vassal treaty? | Yes; he says Sinai's covenant structure resembles Late Bronze Age suzerain-vassal treaty forms. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=6712" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:51:52</a> | How high is 15 cubits in Genesis 7:20? | Assuming the standard 18-inch cubit, about 22.5 feet. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=6882" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:54:42</a> | What are your thoughts on the miniseries *Jesus of Nazareth*? | He does not recall seeing it; he instead comments briefly on other Bible films and series. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=7288" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:01:28</a> | As a historian, how do you react to "those who do not learn from history are doomed to repeat it"? | Knowing history does not guarantee avoiding repetition; history rhymes more than it exactly repeats. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=7823" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:10:23</a> | Do you know good scholarly works on Deuteronomy as forgery, and who was the most formidable ancient king? | He cannot recommend Deuteronomy-forgery works; he names Nebuchadnezzar II and Thutmose III as top candidates. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=8187" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:16:27</a> | What are your thoughts on *The Chosen*'s depiction of Moses/Moshe and its dating? | He thinks they initially got the date right but then backed away from that historical direction. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=8372" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:19:32</a> | Would you recommend Christians read the Book of Enoch? | Only for studying Second Temple literature, not for Scripture-level guidance or new biblical insight. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=8733" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:25:33</a> | What about believers caught up in Bible fan-fiction conspiracy theories around the Nephilim? | Some is harmless, but it can distract from gospel concerns and better interpretations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=9129" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:32:09</a> | What is the most "metal" god other than Yahweh? | He first answers literally with Egyptian divine bodies as metal, then says for heavy-metal style one would look more to Norse gods. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=9356" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:35:56</a> | Why does Moses's staff become one kind of serpent in Exodus 4 but Aaron's another before Pharaoh? | He treats the difference as significant and cautiously explores possible literary and symbolic reasons. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=9916" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:45:16</a> | Do you think Ariak king of Ellasar is Rim-Sin/Ariaku of Larsa, and why use the Sumerian name instead of Akkadian? | He has not done the reading and cannot answer yet; the row preserves that uncertainty. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/ravUgMK9MEU?t=10281" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:51:21</a> | Did the Israelites go through the wilderness in Africa? | He says no, locating the wilderness experience in Sinai rather than Africa more broadly. |
